meta.title: Furano Ski Resort
meta.description: A 28-course mega resort linking Furano, Biei, Tomamu, and Asahikawa.

hero.title:
Furano
Ski Resort
hero.tagline: Mega-resort snow linking four distinct worlds.

triangle.title:
Ski, hills, resort, mountains—
one snow links all four.
triangle.lead: Furano Ski Resort is more than a ski hill: a geographic hub tying Furano (28-course mega resort and Prince Hotels), Biei (patchwork hills and photo culture), Tomamu (Hoshino Resorts ice village), and Asahikawa–Mt. Asahidake (ramen culture and alpine hot springs). Ski as your anchor and blend four distinct experiences seamlessly.
triangle.noticeBody: Public transit between Furano, Biei, and Tomamu is limited—check Route 237 winter conditions and shuttle timetables. "Ramen in Asahikawa → Biei hills → Furano night skiing" needs a car or hotel transfer. Rental cars from Asahikawa Airport offer the most flexibility for multi-base stays.
triangle.imgAlt: Furano snowscape and Kitanomine zone—the corridor hub

primary.title: 28 courses of Japow—inland light powder
primary.lead: Light powder from the Furano and Kitanomine zones, ropeway summit 941 m. Prince Hotels operation with 9 lifts, night skiing, and free elementary passes—one hour from Asahikawa Airport, then Biei hills, Tomamu ice village, and Asahidake hot springs. World-class mega-resort facilities with central Hokkaido calm.
primary.imgAlt: Powder burn in Kitanomine zone—Furano Ski Resort

secondary.title: Two-zone design and night skiing
secondary.lead: Furano zone (ropeway, night skiing) and Kitanomine zone (long cruisers) are clearly split—beginners through experts each get dedicated terrain. Slope-side Prince Hotels deliver ski-in/out and international-grade hospitality.

mobility.title:
Furano Corridor
routing and constraints.
mobility.lead: Routing directly shapes experience quality. Rental cars from Asahikawa Airport are ideal for multi-base loops across Furano, Biei, and Tomamu. Furano Station–resort shuttles (for guests and day-ticket holders) and Tomamu–Furano shuttles lower the barrier of winter driving.

sapporo.note: Hub for ramen village and airport

mobility.rentacarLink: Compare rental cars at Asahikawa Airport
mobility.imgAlt: Route 237 and Tokachi mountains—winter corridor driving

picks.title:
Curated
shortlist.
picks.lead: Stays, onsen, and dining chosen on five axes—snowscape, resort life, local food, onsen comfort, and access. A list you can build a Furano Corridor loop from.

chaletIvy.name: New Furano Prince Hotel
chaletIvy.hook: Flagship slope-side hotel—ski-in/out and scenic baths at the core of mega-resort stays.

kuriyasuizan.name: Furano Natulux Hotel
kuriyasuizan.hook: Designer hotel near Furano Station—base for town strolls and slope access.

yurakusoan.name: Ningle Terrace Hotel
yurakusoan.hook: Log-cabin rooms in the forest—hideaway for Furano's creative culture.

unwind.name: Hoshino Resorts Tomamu The Tower
unwind.hook: Ice village and sea-of-clouds terrace—resort winter entertainment anchor.

omo5.name: OMO7 Asahikawa by Hoshino Resorts
omo5.hook: Gateway to Asahikawa Airport and Ramen Village—urban hub for corridor loops.

hoheikyo.name: Furanui Onsen (Furano Vessel Hotel)
hoheikyo.hook: 100% spring-fed open-air baths—post-ski recovery and town stopover.

koganeyu.name: Tokachidake Onsen Ryounkaku
koganeyu.hook: Sulfur snow-view baths at the foot of Mt. Asahidake—alpine hot-spring peak.

garaku.name: Furano Cheese Factory
garaku.hook: Local dairy soul food—factory tour and tasting staple.

masazushi.name: Furano Kankanhoro
masazushi.hook: Famous omurice and soup curry—gastronomy anchor for locals and visitors alike.

biergarten.name: Asahikawa Ramen Village (Aji no Tokeidai)
biergarten.hook: Northern ramen pilgrimage—easy food hub on corridor return legs.

amenoyuki.name: Biei Farm
amenoyuki.hook: Raclette toast and hill panoramas—where photo culture meets local food.

food.label: Dining
food.title: 4 curated restaurants
food.hook: Kankanhoro, cheese factory, ramen village—food that drives circulation.

tour.title:
Three-base stay,
snow quality at Furano.
tour.lead: Enter via Asahikawa ramen, photograph Biei hills, ski Furano mega resort and night slopes—multi-base design with Tomamu ice village in between. One rental car covers the full corridor.

02.title: Kitanomine zone
02.body: Long cruisers and wide burns—two-zone circuit

03.title: Biei · Patchwork Hills
03.body: Afternoon photo drive—35 min on Route 237

04.title: Tokachidake Onsen Ryounkaku
04.body: Sulfur snow-view bath at the foot of Mt. Asahidake

05.title: Asahikawa · Ramen Village
05.body: Next day: miso ramen, then airport limousine home

triangle.title: Complete Furano Corridor guide
triangle.body: Link Furano, Biei, Tomamu, and Asahikawa through a ski hub. A 28-course mega resort as spine—Japow, hill photography, resort entertainment, and ramen culture in one journey.

mobility.title: Winter roads and multi-base routing
mobility.body: Route 237 winter conditions, limited transit between Furano and Biei, Asahikawa Airport rental logistics—schedules that match infrastructure reality. Confirm hotel shuttles and Tomamu link bus timetables.

culture.title: Mega resort meets local culture
culture.body: Prince Hotels international standards alongside Kankanhoro, cheese factory, and Biei Farm local food—unifying experiences of different scale in one corridor. Multilingual visibility at booking is the competitiveness lever.

access.note: ~60 min from Asahikawa Airport by bus or car · ~60 min from central Asahikawa · ~35 min from Biei · parking available
